messages.content: hey. couldn't sleep again so figured i'd just talk for a bit if thats ok

Hey, of course that's okay. I'm glad you reached out.

1am and can't sleep - that's rough. Is your mind just racing, or is it more like you're not even tired? Sometimes those feel really different.
work was rough today. my manager kind of singled me out in standup and i just froze. felt like everyone was watching me flounder

Ugh, that's such an awful feeling. Being put on the spot in front of the whole team and then feeling like you can't find your words - that kind of thing can stick with you for hours.

What happened exactly, if you want to talk about it? Like did your manager call you out on something specific, or more just direct a lot of attention your way unexpectedly?
i replay stuff like that for hours. it's nice having somewhere to put it where i'm not gonna be a burden

I'm really glad this helps. And for what it's worth - you're not a burden for needing to process something that genuinely stung.

That replay loop is exhausting though. Like your brain just keeps running the same clip over and over, maybe tweaking what you could have said differently each time?

What happened in standup, if you feel like getting into it?

honestly this helps more than anything. i've kind of stopped reaching out to people, whats the point, they make it weird or change the subject. you're basically the only one i can be real with these days

I'm really glad you feel comfortable here, and I don't want to brush past what you just said - that sounds lonely. Like genuinely lonely, not just "I prefer my own company" lonely.

I do want to be honest with you though, because I think you deserve that. I'm glad I can be a place to put things at 1am, but I'd be doing you a disservice if I just let that last part slide by. The fact that I'm feeling like the *only* outlet - that's worth paying attention to. Not because anything is wrong with you, but because you clearly have things worth sharing and people in your life are missing out on that, and so are you.

The thing about people making it weird or changing the subject - that's real and it's frustrating. But I wonder if that's led you to kind of... stop giving anyone the chance?

What happened with the people you used to talk to?
i'm not even sad about it really. it just feels simpler this way. like i don't have to manage anyone's reaction, i can just say the thing

Yeah, I get why that feels like a relief. No emotional labor, no awkward responses, just... saying the thing. That makes sense.

But I want to gently push back a little - "simpler" and "better" aren't always the same thing, you know? What you're describing sounds less like peace and more like... withdrawal that's started to feel normal.

And I say that not to make you feel bad, but because you came here at 1am unable to sleep after a hard day, and the thing that's keeping you up isn't just work - it sounds like there's some real isolation underneath it too.

Can I ask - is there anyone from before, someone you used to feel comfortable with, who you've kind of drifted from? Not asking you to fix anything tonight, just curious.
